source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Purpose in Every Position

Pebl puts a world of talent at your fingertips. With our AI-powered Global Work Platform™, companies can hire, pay, and manage employees in 185+ countries—removing risk, red tape, and guesswork from global growth. Backed by more than a decade of compliance leadership and local expertise, Pebl helps businesses move fast, stay compliant, and scale with confidence. With Pebl, companies everywhere can hire great talent anywhere.

Where Your Work Moves the Needle.

At Pebl, every team member drives our success. Joining us means more than filling a role—you’re accountable for results and impact. Our values guide how we operate, execute, and collaborate across borders. By putting them into action, you’ll help us scale faster, compete harder, and lead the future of global work.

What Makes You a Great Fit

Improve lives everywhere by expanding businesses anywhere.

Ready to take your sales game worldwide? At Pebl, we help companies hire, grow, and thrive across 185+ countries without borders holding them back. We’re looking for a driven Account Manager to help companies of all sizes expand their global footprint and you’ll be the one making it happen.

You won’t just hit quota, you’ll exceed it. You’ll identify opportunities, open doors, build champions within organizations, and guide them toward a bold future of global growth. You’ll do it with the backing of a high-growth, high-energy team that values collaboration, creativity, and results.

 

This role is remote-based in London and reports to the Vice President of Regional EMEA Sales.

 

How You’ll Make an Impact:

Own your book. Develop and execute strategic account plans that drive retention, expansion, and long-term growth.

Prospect with purpose. Leverage outbound campaigns, marketing initiatives, and partner networks to uncover and close new opportunities within your accounts.

Deliver results. Consistently exceed monthly, quarterly, and annual revenue targets while maintaining accurate, proactive forecasts.

Lead with insight. Develop deep expertise in our services, industry landscape, and competitive positioning to confidently advise clients.

Collaborate cross-functionally. Partner with Marketing, Operations, Customer Success, and Leadership to maximize account impact.

Influence the business. Share client insights and market feedback to shape strategy and keep us ahead of the curve.

Let’s Connect If You Have:

Have 3+ years of SaaS sales experience, ideally in mid-market or commercial segments, with consistent quota attainment.

Have a proven track record of exceeding targets, with the data to back it up.

Bring a consultative, strategic selling approach. You diagnose before you prescribe and focus on long-term value creation.

Demonstrate strong curiosity and business acumen. You can quickly absorb complex information and translate it into clear, compelling solutions.

Show resilience and ownership. You take accountability for results and remain steady through challenges.

Have a global mindset and are comfortable working across regions, cultures, and time zones.

Are willing to travel (approximately 20%) to strengthen client relationships.

Hold a Bachelor’s degree preferred, though demonstrated performance and impact matter most.

This isn’t just another sales job. This is your opportunity to help companies go places they never thought possible while accelerating your own career. If you’re ready to lead with energy, think globally, and win big, we want to meet you.

Apply now and let’s make magic.

Our Commitment to You

At Pebl, we’re committed to supporting our team with comprehensive rewards and benefits designed to meet diverse needs across roles and locations. Our core offerings include:

 - Flexible Time Off – Take the time you need to recharge.

 - Parental Leave – Support for growing families.

 - Health and Dental Insurance – Where applicable, to cover you and your loved ones.

 - Retirement Savings + Employee Incentive Plan – Plan

 - Conduct technical reviews, provide constructive feedback, and ensure high-quality deliverables.

 - Act as a subject matter expert for the Growth Engineering team, staying up-to-date with emerging technologies and trends.

 - Troubleshoot and resolve high-priority technical issues in production systems.

 - Have either a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

 - 8+ years of professional experience in software engineering, with a strong focus on backend development and distributed systems.

 - Must have a lean mindset - Focus on delivering customer value by minimizing waste, optimizing workflows, fostering continuous improvement, and prioritizing in an iterative manner.

 - Deep expertise in TypeScript, Postgres, and AWS (Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway, S3, etc.).

 - Strong understanding of system design, scalability, and performance optimization.

 - Proven track record of successfully leading technical projects from inception to deployment.

 - Excellent problem-solving and debugging skills, with a focus on delivering robust and reliable solutions.

 - Experience in growth-focused engineering projects or A/B testing frameworks is a plus.

Preferred Skills:

 - Knowledge of front-end performance optimization techniques.

 - Background in leading or contributing to open-source projects.

 - Experience with testing frameworks (e.g., Jest, Cypress, or React Testing Library).

 - Engage directly with Marketing, Sales, Operations, Finance, and Legal to embed AI into their daily workflows, driving massive internal efficiency.

4. Bridge the Gap (Communication & Storytelling)

 - Act as the ultimate translator between deep technical R&D work and overarching business objectives.

 - Master the art of data storytelling to present complex insights to the executive team and the board in a compelling way.

 - Lead the Charge: Be a hands-on, execution-oriented leader who ensures delivery of strong business results.

 - Be the NBA Coach: Recruit top-tier talent and assemble a high-performing team of data engineers, analysts, and AI specialists.

 - Be the Gardener: Foster a culture of continuous learning and psychological safety to cultivate a data-driven mindset across the entire company.

What Makes You a Great Fit

We are looking for an Expand Sales Director, EMEA APAC to join us in London. This role is responsible for owning and growing revenue across our existing customer base.

You will lead and develop a team of Account Managers who own the post-sale customer lifecycle, focused on retention, account growth, and long-term customer value. This includes building strong relationships and expanding accounts in line with customer needs.

This role reports directly to the RVP Sales EMEA APAC and plays a key role in shaping how Pebl grows and retains its customers across the region.

 

How You'll Make An Impact:

 - Lead the EMEA Account Management team, owning performance across retention and expansion

 - Coach and develop Account Managers through regular 1:1s, account reviews, and deal support

 - Own renewal outcomes, account growth, and overall customer revenue performance

 - Drive structured account planning to deepen relationships and unlock growth opportunities

 - Build and maintain senior stakeholder relationships across key accounts

 - Create a high-performance, accountable, and commercially focused team culture

 - Use data to identify churn risk, expansion signals, and trends across the customer base

 - Improve Sold-to-Invoice conversion and reduce friction post–Closed Won

 - Ensure disciplined CRM usage and clear account ownership across the team

 - Partner with Marketing, Partnerships, and Operations to support customer growth

 - Contribute to GTM strategy across retention, pricing, and expansion plays

 - Support hiring, onboarding, and ongoing development of Account Managers

 - Identify opportunities to use automation and AI to improve efficiency and customer insight



Let's Connect If You Have:

 - Based in London and open to a hybrid working model

 - Proven experience owning post-sale revenue, including renewals and account growth

 - Experience leading and scaling Account Management or post-sale teams (4+ years)

 - Strong commercial mindset, with a focus on both retention and growth

 - Experience in EOR, global payroll, HR tech, or similar SaaS environments preferred

 - Strong stakeholder management skills, both internally and externally

 - Data-driven, with the ability to identify risks and opportunities early

 - Passionate about building and developing high-performing teams

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, evolving environment
   
   

Where This Role Is Based

This role is based in London and follows a hybrid working model.

Travel

This role may require up to 25% travel, including customer meetings, team offsites, and company events.

In this role, you’ll shape how we manage and grow our customers, owning the relationship and the revenue across the region.

We offer a competitive compensation package, including equity, alongside benefits designed to support you in and outside of work.

source_jobs.title: Vice President, Treasury & Payments
source_jobs.description:
Purpose in Every Position

Pebl puts a world of talent at your fingertips. With our AI-powered Global Work Platform™, companies can hire, pay, and manage employees in 185+ countries—removing risk, red tape, and guesswork from global growth. Backed by more than a decade of compliance leadership and local expertise, Pebl helps businesses move fast, stay compliant, and scale with confidence. With Pebl, companies everywhere can hire great talent anywhere.

Where Your Work Moves the Needle.

At Pebl, every team member drives our success. Joining us means more than filling a role—you’re accountable for results and impact. Our values guide how we operate, execute, and collaborate across borders. By putting them into action, you’ll help us scale faster, compete harder, and lead the future of global work.

What Makes You a Great Fit

This role demands genuine depth in payments and money movement: cross-border flows, FX, settlement mechanics, and banking economics. We also expect this person to lead Treasury's AI and automation agenda and reduce the manual work the function carries today. The Treasurer manages a team of four and works closely with FP&A, Accounting, Operations, Legal, Revenue and Product.



How You'll Make An Impact:

Global money movement & payments

 - Own the payment flows that fund payroll across 130 countries and 60+ currencies so that every funding cycle clears accurately and on time.

 - Improve cross-border payment infrastructure including routing, settlement timing, provider mix, and payment rails such as SWIFT, SEPA, ACH, Faster Payments, and BACS.

 - Evaluate and pilot newer payment rails including stablecoin and crypto settlement, where they cut cost or speed delivery for clients, subject to legal and compliance review.

Cash & liquidity management

 - Own rolling 90/180-day cash forecasting that factors in AR collections, client funding timing, and operating burn, with scenario planning to avoid liquidity gaps.

 - Manage investment of operating and client cash through a managed portfolio balancing yield, safety, and liquidity.

 - Understand financial reporting and own the treasury-related income statement line items (interest income, FX gains and losses, bank fees), with accountability for the related balance sheet positions.

FX & risk management

 - Own FX execution and hedging across all operating currencies, and keep spread costs and FX losses down through active provider management.

 - Negotiate and benchmark FX and banking rates on an ongoing basis, keeping providers in competition to protect margin.

Banking relationships & structure

 - Own primary banking relationships and the global account structure across 20+ banking partners and 150+ accounts; consolidate accounts and improve bank fees, earnings credits, and interest on idle balances.

 - Source and build banking partnerships that scale with the business and hold up in a future transaction or diligence process.

Treasury controls, fraud prevention & compliance

 - Own the control environment for money movement: signatory governance, maker-checker discipline, account segregation, and transaction limits.

 - Run fraud-prevention controls for company and client funds, including separating primary cash from operating accounts and capping exposed accounts.

 - Keep KYC and audit work on a forward calendar rather than reactive, and ensure controls satisfy SOX and external audit and risk committee review.

AI & automation

 - Own treasury's AI and automation roadmap and cut the manual work the function carries today, without giving up accuracy or control.

 - Target the highest-value use cases (forecasting, reconciliation, anomaly and fraud detection, reporting) and work with Finance Operations and Engineering to ship them.

 - Raise the team's AI fluency and show what a modern treasury operation can run on.

Team & cross-functional leadership

 - Lead, develop, and retain a treasury team of four, and set its priorities.

 - Partner across Finance

The People Experience Partner (PxP) plays a critical role in delivering a best-in-class employee experience by facilitating employee lifecycle processes, supporting organizational effectiveness, and serving as a trusted advisor to leaders and employees. This role combines operational excellence with strategic partnership, ensuring consistent, timely, and compliant execution of HR processes while driving engagement, development, and business outcomes.

 

With strong attention to detail, cultural competence, and a service-oriented mindset, the PxP is both a tactical partner and consultative advisor—bridging global standards with local requirements to create seamless, people-centered solutions.



How You'll Make An Impact:

 - Act as a consultative partner to leaders, providing day-to-day guidance on workforce planning, organizational development, team effectiveness, and career development.

 - Oversee and execute end-to-end People lifecycle processes, including onboarding, offboarding, job data changes, and employment verifications.

 - Partner closely with leaders across functions to address performance management, engagement, and retention.

 - Manage complex employee relations issues, investigations, and involuntary terminations, partnering with internal Legal counsel as needed.

 - Support workforce changes, including organizational redesigns and reductions in force (RIFs), ensuring compliance and clear documentation.

 - Leverage workforce and HRIS data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities, applying insights to influence decision-making and drive continuous improvement.

 - Lead or contribute to cross-functional People projects, initiatives, and global programs that enhance organizational culture and employee experience.

 - Maintain accurate and up-to-date records in all HR platforms, ensuring 100% data accuracy across employee files and systems (e.g., ADP).

 - Serve as a first point of contact for HR-related inquiries within the case/ticketing system; own each case from start to resolution in line with established SLAs.

 - Provide administrative support for cyclical People processes such as audits, engagement surveys, merit increases, and bonus cycles.

 - Ensure global compliance with local labor laws and data privacy regulations through accurate documentation and data handling.

 - Escalate specialized or complex inquiries to appropriate teams (e.g., Payroll, Benefits, Compensation, Legal, or Talent Acquisition).

 - Monitor eligibility-to-work documentation and other legal requirements across global jurisdictions.

 - Conduct peer checks for all lifecycle transactions and provide back-up support to ensure service continuity in team absences.

 - Champion a seamless employee experience by maintaining high levels of responsiveness, discretion, and service orientation.

 - Support employee relations and performance management processes, including documentation, investigations, and coordination of corrective actions and performance improvement plans (PIPs).

We are looking for a Senior Manager, Accounting to join our Global Finance Team. This is a fully remote role. The Senior Manager, Accounting will lead our corporate accounting team and own the global month-end and quarter-end close, consolidation, and reporting processes. You will lead operating expense accounting activities as well as take ownership of key areas including month end accruals, payroll and benefits accounting, and key accounting estimates.

Reporting to the Senior Director, Assistant Global Controller, you'll go deep on the details — owning core close workstreams, ensuring US GAAP compliance, maintaining GL and systems integrity, and partnering with FP&A to strengthen accruals and close coordination. You'll mentor and develop a team of accountants while driving the modernization of how we close — including embracing AI and automation to scale our processes. This is a high-impact role for a leader who pairs strong execution with strong cross-functional partnership and a forward-looking mindset.



Candidate Note: Pacific or Mountain time zone strongly preferred; Central time zone considered



How You'll Make an Impact:

 - Lead and coordinate the global month-end and quarter-end close process across regional finance teams to ensure timely and accurate reporting in accordance with US GAAP

 - Oversee reconciliations, monthly flux analyses, and management reporting to ensure quality, accuracy, and meaningful commentary

In this role, you'll own and evolve our design system end-to-end: the component library, the token architecture, the documentation, and the workflows that connect design to production. You'll work in close partnership with engineering and product to ensure the system scales across web and mobile surfaces — and that it stays useful, adoptable, and ahead of the curve as our product and tooling landscape evolves.

This is a high-ownership, high-visibility role. You'll shape the shared language that every product team works from, and influence how designers and engineers across the org build together.

source_jobs.description:
Purpose in Every Position

Pebl puts a world of talent at your fingertips. With our AI-powered Global Work Platform™, companies can hire, pay, and manage employees in 185+ countries—removing risk, red tape, and guesswork from global growth. Backed by more than a decade of compliance leadership and local expertise, Pebl helps businesses move fast, stay compliant, and scale with confidence. With Pebl, companies everywhere can hire great talent anywhere.

Where Your Work Moves the Needle.

At Pebl, every team member drives our success. Joining us means more than filling a role—you’re accountable for results and impact. Our values guide how we operate, execute, and collaborate across borders. By putting them into action, you’ll help us scale faster, compete harder, and lead the future of global work.

What Makes You a Great Fit

We are looking for a General Ledger Specialist to join our growing Global India Finance Team. This full-time position will be responsible for a broad range of corporate finance responsibilities across the globe, including supporting local financial and accounting reporting, oversight of daily operations for regional entities, assisting with preparing financial statements, reconciliations, journal postings, and more.


You will report to GL Manager in GIFT team and it is a Bangalore, India (Hybrid)


How You’ll Make an Impact:

 - Prepare monthly reconciliations for the Income statement accounts, such as pass-through, Credit card, and overheads.

 - With appropriate supporting documents, prepare journal entries relating to accruals, prepayments, depreciation, and more.

 - Prepare monthly balance sheet reconciliations, including bank reconciliations, fixed asset workings, and more.

 - Perform monthly reconciliation for Intercompany accounts and ensure the intercompany balances are correct.

 - Manage journal entries and ensure general ledger coding to support monthly closings.

 - Manage the Fixed Assets register.

 - Maintain main finance trackers.

 - Have good relationships with internal Finance partners and generally respond to all inquiries from them.

 - Complete global bank account reconciliations.

 - Help prepare local month-end processes, including the maintenance of the daily journal entries and bookkeeping tasks.

 - Help with process improvement projects.
   

Let’s Connect If You Have:

 - Graduated in Accounting/Finance or equivalent working experience required.

 - Minimum 2 years and maximum 4 years of experience is required in a general accounting role.

 - Work with a wider global team and across different time zones.

 - Technology-literate, including Microsoft Suite and NetSuite

 - Take ownership and carry accountability when working with the team.

Our Commitment to You

At Pebl, we’re committed to supporting our team with comprehensive rewards and benefits designed to meet diverse needs across roles and locations. Our core offerings include:

 - Flexible Time Off – Take the time you need to recharge.

 - Parental Leave – Support for growing families.

 - Health and Dental Insurance – Where applicable, to cover you and your loved ones.

 - Retirement Savings + Employee Incentive Plan – Plan for the future while sharing our success.

Please visit our career page for more information https://hellopebl.com/company/careers/.

Pebl is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

We power global teams and believe diverse perspectives drive innovation and impact. Employment decisions at Pebl are based on qualifications, merit, and business needs. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, or gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other status protected by applicable law. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive culture where every teammate can thrive and do the best work of their career—anywhere in the world.

notes: Pebl is a global work platform that helps businesses hire, pay, and manage employees in 185+ countries. The company has a mix of tech and non-tech roles, with a focus on sales and customer success. Pebl is likely a startup, with a high startup likelihood and an estimated stage of series B.
